FULL CONTENT:
![Logo Heidelberg Materials](https://www.heidelbergmaterials.co.uk/sites/default/files/logo/HeidelbergMaterials.svg)
![](/sites/default/files/assets/images/9a/58/ref-kincardine-bridge.jpg)

# Clackmannanshire Bridge

**Project summary**

**Product and volume supplied by Hanson:**

**More about Regen**

Regen is Ground Granulated Blast furnace Slag, commonly known as ‘GGBS’.

It is a cement substitute, manufactured from a by-product of the iron-making industry.

Using one tonne of Regen in concrete reduces the embodied CO2 by around 850kg, compared to using one tonne of Portland Cement. It also increases the concrete’s durability and is more sustainable than other cement substitutes such as Fly Ash.

In the UK, GGBS is usually supplied as a separate component for concrete and is added at the concrete mixer.

It can replace 70 per cent or more of the Portland cement. In British Standards, GGBS is referred to as an ‘addition’ and counts fully towards the cement content in concrete.

Regen conforms to BS EN 15167-1 “Ground Granulated Blast furnace Slag for use in concrete, mortar and grout” and is available throughout the UK, accompanied by a full technical and logistical support package.

[Read more case studies on Hanson's use of Regen GGBS](https://www.hanson.co.uk/en/sector-case-studies)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Projects like Clackmannanshire Bridge highlight GGBS's role as a sustainable alternative, significantly reducing CO2 emissions while enhancing concrete durability and performance.
Research indicates that GGBS concrete exhibits superior resistance to chloride and sulfate attacks, contributing to extended service life in marine and aggressive environments.
GGBS conforms to international standards, such as BS EN 15167-1, ensuring its acceptance in concrete applications and supporting sustainable construction practices.
Feedback from stakeholders emphasizes GGBS's cost-effectiveness and environmental benefits, reinforcing its importance in modern sustainable construction methodologies.
